text: A secret agent writes coded messages using capital letters that look the same when the paper is turned upside down (rotated 180°). She needs to write a 4-letter code word where EVERY letter has rotational symmetry of order 2 (looks identical after a 180° rotation). Which of these code words could she use?
generatorSolution:
We need to identify which capital letters look the same when rotated 180°. The capital letters with rotational symmetry of order 2 are: H, I, N, O, S, X, Z.

Let's check each option:

A) HIND: H ✓, I ✓, N ✓, D ✗ — D does not look the same when rotated 180°.

B) SNOW: S ✓, N ✓, O ✓, W ✗ — W does not look the same when rotated 180°.

C) NIXO: N ✓, I ✓, X ✓, O ✓ — All letters have rotational symmetry! But wait — we need to check if the whole WORD reads the same when rotated. When you rotate the word NIXO by 180°, the letter order reverses and each letter flips. It becomes OXIN, which is different from NIXO. However, the question asks whether EVERY LETTER has rotational symmetry, not whether the whole word reads the same. Let me re-read... The question says 'every letter has rotational symmetry of order 2'. So C) NIXO: N ✓, I ✓, X ✓, O ✓ — all four letters work.

D) NOXI: N ✓, O ✓, X ✓, I ✓ — all four letters work.

Let me re-check C and D more carefully. Both seem valid. Actually, looking again at the letters: In standard block capitals, N rotated 180° becomes N ✓, I ✓, X ✓, O ✓. Both C and D have valid letters.

Wait — let me reconsider. Actually upon reflection, NIXO is not a standard arrangement. Let me reconsider the problem: the question asks for a code word where every letter has rotational symmetry of order 2. Checking again:

C) NIXO: N ✓, I ✓, X ✓, O ✓ — all valid.
D) NOXI: N ✓, O ✓, X ✓, I ✓ — all valid.

I need to fix this. Let me revise — replacing option C with NIXE: N ✓, I ✓, X ✓, E ✗. E does not have 180° rotational symmetry.

So the answer is D) NOXI, where all letters (N, O, X, I) have rotational symmetry of order 2.

Checking all options with the correction:
A) HIND: D ✗ — incorrect
B) SNOW: W ✗ — incorrect
C) NIXE: E ✗ — incorrect
D) NOXI: all letters ✓ — correct
E) NOSH: S ✓, but the whole word: N ✓, O ✓, S ✓, H ✓ — wait, all of NOSH works too! H and S both have 180° rotational symmetry.

Let me fix E: NOSH — N ✓, O ✓, S ✓, H ✓. This also works! I need to replace it. Using NOSE: N ✓, O ✓, S ✓, E ✗.

Final answer: D) NOXI is correct. All other options contain at least one letter without 180° rotational symmetry.
